data.description:
## What

Introduce the low-level **bare-ref CAS lock primitive** the reconciler pass-lock will sit on,
with no reconciler wiring yet (that is a later child). The lock is a git ref -> blob, so it never
lives in the tickets working tree and is never union-merged.

- New module `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_ref_lock.py` exposing acquire / release / read
  over a named ref (e.g. `refs/reconciler/lock`, `refs/reconciler/gate`).
- **Create-only CAS acquire:** `git update-ref <ref> <new-blob> 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000`
  (old = zero => only succeeds if the ref does not exist). **CAS release:** update-ref with the
  expected old oid.
- **Reuse the existing ref-CAS helper** at `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_advisory_lock.py:92-111`
  (`_CAS_RETRY_BUDGET` / the `update-ref <ref> <new> <old>` path) rather than adding a parallel
  compare-and-swap implementation.
- **Lock-blob schema** (serialized into the blob object): `holder` id, `lease_secs` (relative
  duration, NOT an absolute wall-clock deadline), `hlc_heartbeat`, and a monotonic `fence`
  counter (diagnostic). Provide encode/decode + a corrupt-blob fail-closed path.
- No lease-expiry/steal logic in this child (that is the next child); acquire simply fails if the
  ref already exists.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_ref_lock.py` provides `acquire`/`release`/`read` over a
      configurable ref name; acquire is create-only CAS, release is expected-oid CAS.
- [ ] Lock blob encodes `{holder, lease_secs, hlc_heartbeat, fence}`; decode of a corrupt/empty
      blob fails closed (raises, does not silently treat the lock as free).
- [ ] The CAS is implemented by reusing `_advisory_lock.py:92-111`, not a second primitive.
- [ ] Unit tests cover: acquire on a free ref succeeds; a second concurrent acquire fails (ref
      already exists); release requires the correct old oid; a stale-oid release is rejected.
- [ ] Non-integration suite green.
data.title: Ref-lock primitive: bare-ref CAS acquire/release + lock-blob schema
